history.label: 20th century
history.text: At the start of the 20th century, Djibouti had 10,000 inhabitants and was considered a major regional port.
On 12 July 1926, the Fontainebleau, a Messageries Maritimes steamer loaded with cotton and heading for China caught fire while approaching Djibouti.

When Djibouti declared Independence on 27 June 1977, the population of Djibouti was over 110,000, the city has since served as the administrative and commercial capital of the Republic of Djibouti.
